title: Accelerate
subtitle: The Science of Lean Software and DevOps
author: Nicole Forsgren, Jez Humble & Gene Kim
themes: The Four Key Metrics: lead time, deployment frequency, MTTR, change failure rate
Elite vs low-performing teams: the 5x / 2,555x performance gap
24 Technical Capabilities that drive software delivery performance
Architecture: loosely coupled vs tightly coupled and delivery outcomes
Lean management practices: limit WIP, monitor flow, feedback loops
Psychological safety and burnout prevention as hard performance drivers
Culture as the root cause of high or low performance
Automated testing, CI, and CD pipelines as foundations
awards: Shingo Research and Professional Publication Award Nominee
DORA research cited in Google's State of DevOps Reports 2014–2018
excerpt: Accelerate presents the findings of four years of research by Nicole Forsgren, Jez Humble, and Gene Kim, demonstrating that software delivery performance can be measured with four key metrics and that specific technical capabilities and cultural practices reliably drive elite performance — validated by 23,000+ survey responses and rigorous statistical analysis.
